chapters.summary: Mole concept, stoichiometry, laws of chemical combination — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Nature of matter; Dalton's atomic theory; concept of atom, molecule, element, compound.
Law of conservation of mass, constant proportions, multiple proportions, reciprocal proportions, Gay-Lussac's law of gaseous volumes.
Atomic mass units; molecular mass; formula mass; average atomic mass.
Avogadro's number; mole as a counting unit; molar mass; interconversion of moles, mass, and number of particles.
% composition from molecular formula; empirical formula from % composition; relation between empirical and molecular formula.
Balancing chemical equations; stoichiometric calculations; limiting reagent; percentage yield; reactions in solution (molarity).

chapters.summary: Quantum numbers, electronic configuration, Bohr model — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Nature of EM radiation; wave properties; photoelectric effect (connects to Physics Unit 17 as well).
Postulates; derivation of energy of electron and radii of orbits; hydrogen spectrum (spectral series); limitations of Bohr's model.

topics.topicName: Dual Nature and Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle
topics.explanation: de Broglie relation (λ = h/mv); Heisenberg's uncertainty principle (Δx·Δp ≥ h/4π).
Quantum mechanics approach; atomic orbitals as one-electron wave functions; variation of ψ and ψ² with r for 1s and 2s; probability density.
Principal (n), azimuthal (l), magnetic (m) and spin (s) quantum numbers; significance; permissible values; shapes of s, p, d orbitals.
Aufbau principle; Pauli's exclusion principle; Hund's rule; filling order; extra stability of half-filled and completely filled orbitals (Cr, Cu exceptions).

chapters.summary: VSEPR, hybridization, MOT — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Kossel-Lewis approach; formation of ionic bonds; factors affecting formation; lattice enthalpy calculation.
Electronegativity; Fajan's rules for polarisation; dipole moment; polar and non-polar molecules.
Valence shell electron pair repulsion; predicting shapes of molecules (linear, trigonal planar, tetrahedral, pyramidal, bent, octahedral etc.).
VBT features; hybridization involving s, p, d orbitals (sp, sp², sp³, sp³d, sp³d²); geometry and bond angles; resonance.

MOT features; LCAOs; bonding and antibonding MOs; sigma and pi bonds; MO electronic configurations of homonuclear diatomics (H₂ to Ne₂); bond order, bond length, bond energy; paramagnetic/diamagnetic character.
Elementary idea of metallic bonding; hydrogen bonding (intermolecular and intramolecular); effect on physical properties and applications.

chapters.summary: First and second laws, Gibbs energy, Hess's law — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: System, surroundings, boundary; extensive vs intensive properties; state functions (U, H, S, G); types of processes (isothermal, adiabatic, reversible, irreversible).
Internal energy; heat (q) and work (w); ΔU = q + w; enthalpy H = U + PV; ΔH at constant pressure; heat capacity (Cv, Cp); molar heat capacity.
Hess's law of constant heat summation; standard enthalpies of bond dissociation, combustion, formation, atomization, sublimation, phase transition, hydration, ionization, and solution.
Spontaneity criteria; entropy (ΔS); ΔS of universe; Gibbs energy ΔG = ΔH - TΔS; standard Gibbs energy (ΔG°) and equilibrium constant (ΔG° = -RT ln K).

chapters.summary: Concentration terms, Raoult's law, colligative properties, van't Hoff factor — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Molality, molarity, mole fraction, normality, % by volume and % by mass; interconversions.
Raoult's law; ideal and non-ideal solutions; positive and negative deviations; vapour pressure-composition plots; azeotropes.
Relative lowering of vapour pressure; elevation of boiling point (ΔTb = Kb × m); depression of freezing point (ΔTf = Kf × m); osmotic pressure (π = CRT); determination of molecular mass.
Abnormal molar mass; van't Hoff factor (i); effect of association and dissociation on colligative properties.

chapters.summary: Le Chatelier's principle, pH, buffer, Ksp — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Dynamic equilibrium; physical process equilibria (solid-liquid, liquid-gas, solid-gas); Henry's law.
Law of chemical equilibrium; Kc and Kp expressions; relationship between Kp and Kc; ΔG and ΔG° in equilibrium; reaction quotient (Q).
Effect of concentration, pressure, temperature, and catalyst on equilibrium position; applications (Haber process, Contact process).
Ionization of electrolytes; Arrhenius, Bronsted-Lowry, Lewis concepts; ionization constants (Ka, Kb); ionization of water; pH scale; multistage ionization.
pH of strong/weak acids and bases; common ion effect; hydrolysis of salts; pH of salt solutions.
Buffer solutions (Henderson-Hasselbalch equation); solubility of sparingly soluble salts; Ksp and its applications; selective precipitation.

chapters.summary: Nernst equation, Faraday's laws, conductance, electrochemical cells — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Official syllabus combines Redox and Electrochemistry into one unit.

topics.topicName: Oxidation Number and Balancing Redox Reactions
topics.explanation: Electronic concepts of oxidation/reduction; oxidation number rules; balancing by oxidation number method and ion-electron (half-reaction) method.

Electrolytic vs metallic conduction; conductance, conductivity, molar conductivity; variation with concentration; Kohlrausch's law and applications (limiting molar conductivity, degree of ionization).
Galvanic and electrolytic cells; electrode types; standard electrode potential (SHE reference); half-cell and full cell reactions; EMF of galvanic cell measurement; cell notation.
Nernst equation: E = E° - (RT/nF) ln Q; calculation of cell EMF at non-standard conditions; relationship between E_cell and ΔG; ΔG = -nFE.
First and second laws of electrolysis; mass deposited calculations; Faraday's constant; applications.
Dry cell (Leclanche cell); lead storage battery; fuel cells (H₂-O₂ fuel cell); working and reactions.

chapters.summary: Rate laws, order of reaction, Arrhenius equation, half-life — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Average and instantaneous rate; factors affecting rate (concentration, temperature, pressure, catalyst); rate expression.
Elementary and complex reactions; order vs molecularity; rate law; rate constant and its units; determining order from data.
Differential and integrated forms of zero and first order reactions; characteristics; half-life (t½ = 0.693/k for first order).
Effect of temperature on rate; Arrhenius equation k = Ae^(-Ea/RT); activation energy calculation from slope of ln k vs 1/T; collision theory of bimolecular gaseous reactions (no derivation).

chapters.chapterName: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
chapters.summary: Periodic trends — 2 questions annually in NEET.

topics.topicName: Modern Periodic Law and the Periodic Table
topics.explanation: Modern periodic law; present form of periodic table; s, p, d, f block classification; periods and groups.
Atomic and ionic radii (across period and down group); ionization enthalpy (1st, 2nd IE); electron gain enthalpy; electronegativity; valence and oxidation states; chemical reactivity trends.

chapters.summary: Groups 13 to 18 — 4-5 questions annually in NEET. One single unit covers all p-block groups 13–18 in official NEET 2026 syllabus.

topics.explanation: Electronic configuration; general trends in physical and chemical properties across periods and down groups; unique behaviour of the first element in each group (anomalous properties).
Properties of boron and aluminium; electron deficiency; back bonding in BF₃; borax, boric acid; diborane; alums.
Allotropes of carbon (diamond, graphite, fullerene); catenation; oxides of carbon (CO, CO₂); silicates; silicon carbide.
Properties; oxidation states; hydrides (NH₃, PH₃); oxoacids of nitrogen and phosphorus; allotropes of phosphorus.
Properties; allotropes of sulphur; hydrides (H₂O, H₂S); oxoacids of sulphur (H₂SO₄, H₂SO₃).
Properties; oxidising power trend; halogen acids (HF, HCl, HBr, HI); interhalogen compounds; oxoacids of chlorine.
Electronic configuration; inertness; xenon compounds (XeF₂, XeF₄, XeF₆, XeO₃); uses.

chapters.summary: Transition elements, KMnO₄, K₂Cr₂O₇, lanthanoids — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Electronic configuration; general trends in first-row transition elements: physical properties, ionization enthalpy, variable oxidation states, atomic radii, colour, catalytic behaviour, magnetic properties, complex formation, interstitial compounds, alloy formation.
Preparation, properties and uses of potassium dichromate (K₂Cr₂O₇) and potassium permanganate (KMnO₄); oxidising reactions in acidic medium.
Electronic configuration; oxidation states; lanthanoid contraction and its consequences on properties of 5d series.
Electronic configuration; oxidation states; comparison with lanthanoids.

chapters.summary: IUPAC nomenclature, isomerism, CFT — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Werner's theory; ligands (types: mono/bi/polydentate, chelating, ambidentate); coordination number; denticity; chelation.
Naming mononuclear coordination compounds: ligand naming order, central metal oxidation state; naming cationic, anionic, neutral complexes.
Structural isomerism (ionisation, hydrate, linkage, coordination); stereoisomerism (geometrical — cis/trans in square planar and octahedral; optical — enantiomers).

topics.topicName: Bonding — VBT and Crystal Field Theory
topics.explanation: Valence bond approach (inner and outer orbital complexes); basic ideas of Crystal Field Theory (CFT); crystal field splitting (Δo, Δt); high spin vs low spin; colour and magnetic properties explanation.
Role in qualitative analysis; extraction of metals (gold, silver); biological systems (haemoglobin, chlorophyll, vitamin B12).

chapters.chapterName: Purification and Characterisation of Organic Compounds
chapters.summary: Purification methods, qualitative and quantitative analysis — 1-2 questions annually in NEET. Was completely MISSING from previous metadata.

topics.explanation: Crystallization; sublimation; distillation (simple, fractional, steam, vacuum); differential extraction; chromatography (TLC, column, paper) — principles and applications.
Detection of nitrogen (Lassaigne's test — sodium fusion), sulphur, phosphorus, and halogens in organic compounds.
Basic principles of estimation of C, H (combustion), N (Dumas/Kjeldahl), halogens (Carius), S, P; calculation of empirical and molecular formulae; numerical problems.

chapters.chapterName: Some Basic Principles of Organic Chemistry
chapters.summary: IUPAC naming, electronic effects, reaction intermediates — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Tetravalency; shapes of simple molecules; sp, sp², sp³ hybridization; classification of organic compounds based on functional groups.
Homologous series; structural isomerism (chain, position, functional, metamerism, tautomerism); stereoisomerism (geometrical, optical).
IUPAC and trivial names; functional group priority rules; naming of compounds with multiple functional groups; substituent naming.
Homolytic and heterolytic fission; free radicals (stability order); carbocations (stability: 3° > 2° > 1°); carbanions; electrophiles and nucleophiles.
Inductive effect (+I, -I); electromeric effect; resonance/mesomeric effect; hyperconjugation — all with examples of effect on reactivity and acidity/basicity.
Substitution (SN1, SN2, electrophilic, free-radical); addition; elimination; rearrangement — classification and overview.

chapters.summary: Alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, arenes — 2-3 questions annually in NEET. Was completely MISSING from previous metadata.

topics.explanation: Classification; IUPAC nomenclature; conformations (Sawhorse and Newman projections of ethane — staggered vs eclipsed); mechanism of halogenation (free radical chain mechanism).
Geometrical isomerism (cis-trans, E-Z); electrophilic addition mechanism; addition of H₂, halogens, water, HX (Markownikoff's rule and peroxide/anti-Markownikoff effect); ozonolysis; polymerization.
Acidic character of terminal alkynes; addition of H₂, halogens, water (Markownikoff's rule), HX; polymerization.

Benzene structure and aromaticity (Hückel's rule — 4n+2 π electrons); mechanism of electrophilic aromatic substitution: halogenation, nitration, Friedel-Crafts alkylation and acylation; directive influence of functional groups in monosubstituted benzene (o/p vs m-directors).

chapters.summary: Haloalkanes, haloarenes, SN1/SN2 — 2-3 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: General methods of preparation; nature of C-X bond (polarity); physical and chemical properties.
SN1 mechanism (carbocation stability, racemization); SN2 mechanism (backside attack, inversion of configuration, steric effects); factors governing SN1 vs SN2.
Preparation; reactions; resonance in haloarenes; decreased reactivity towards nucleophilic substitution; electrophilic substitution (o/p-direction by halogens).
Uses of haloalkanes; environmental effects of chloroform, iodoform, freons (ozone depletion), and DDT (biomagnification).

chapters.summary: Alcohols, phenols, aldehydes, ketones, carboxylic acids — 3-4 questions annually in NEET. Note: Official syllabus covers Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers, Aldehydes, Ketones AND Carboxylic Acids all in one unit.

topics.explanation: Identification of 1°, 2°, 3° alcohols (Lucas test); mechanism of dehydration (E1); esterification; oxidation of alcohols.
Acidic nature (resonance stabilisation of phenoxide ion); acidity comparison; electrophilic substitution: halogenation, nitration, sulphonation; Reimer-Tiemann reaction; Kolbe's reaction.
Structure of ethers; preparation (Williamson synthesis); properties; cleavage reactions.

Nature of carbonyl group; nucleophilic addition to C=O: addition of HCN, NaHSO₃, NH₃ and derivatives (hydroxylamine, semicarbazide, 2,4-DNP), Grignard reagent; oxidation; reduction (Wolff-Kishner and Clemmensen); acidity of α-hydrogen; aldol condensation; Cannizzaro reaction; haloform reaction; chemical tests to distinguish aldehydes from ketones.
Acidic strength and factors affecting it (inductive effect of substituents, resonance); comparison of acidity of substituted carboxylic acids.

chapters.summary: Amines, diazonium salts — 1-2 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Nomenclature; classification (1°, 2°, 3° aliphatic and aromatic); structure; basic character (aliphatic > ammonia > aromatic amines); factors affecting basicity; identification of 1°/2°/3° amines (Hinsberg test, Carbylamine test).
Preparation from primary aromatic amines; importance in synthetic organic chemistry: Sandmeyer reaction, Balz-Schiemann reaction, coupling reactions (azo dyes).

chapters.summary: Carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, nucleic acids, hormones — 3-4 questions annually in NEET.

topics.explanation: Classification (mono-, di-, polysaccharides); aldoses and ketoses; monosaccharides: glucose (open chain and ring structures, anomers) and fructose; disaccharides: sucrose, lactose, maltose (constituent monosaccharides and glycosidic linkage); polysaccharides: starch, cellulose, glycogen.

α-amino acids (essential and non-essential); peptide bond formation; polypeptides; protein structure: primary (sequence), secondary (α-helix, β-pleated sheet), tertiary (3D folding), quaternary (association of subunits); denaturation of proteins; enzymes (characteristics and specificity).
Classification: fat-soluble (A, D, E, K) and water-soluble (B complex, C); deficiency diseases associated with each vitamin.

Chemical constitution of DNA (deoxyribose, phosphate, bases: A, T, G, C) and RNA (ribose, phosphate, bases: A, U, G, C); nucleoside vs nucleotide; biological functions (storage and transfer of genetic information, protein synthesis).
General introduction; classification (steroid, peptide, amino acid-derived hormones); examples and their roles.

chapters.summary: Salt analysis, organic detection, preparations, titrations — 1-2 questions annually in NEET. Was completely MISSING from previous metadata.

topics.topicName: Detection of Elements in Organic Compounds
topics.explanation: Detection of nitrogen, sulphur, and halogens using sodium fusion (Lassaigne's test); detection of phosphorus.

Detection of: hydroxyl (alcoholic — Lucas test/esterification; phenolic — FeCl₃ test); carbonyl (aldehyde — Tollens, Fehling; ketone — 2,4-DNP); carboxyl (NaHCO₃ test); amino (Hinsberg test, Carbylamine test).
Preparation of Mohr's salt [FeSO₄·(NH₄)₂SO₄·6H₂O] and Potash alum [KAl(SO₄)₂·12H₂O] — chemistry involved.
Chemistry involved in preparation of: acetanilide, p-nitroacetanilide, aniline yellow (dye), iodoform.
Acid-base titrations with indicators; oxalic acid vs KMnO₄ (redox titration); Mohr's salt vs KMnO₄ — chemistry principles involved.

Cation detection: Pb²⁺, Cu²⁺, Al³⁺, Fe³⁺, Zn²⁺, Ni²⁺, Ca²⁺, Ba²⁺, Mg²⁺, NH₄⁺ (group separation and confirmatory tests). Anion detection: CO₃²⁻, S²⁻, SO₄²⁻, NO₃⁻, NO₂⁻, Cl⁻, Br⁻, I⁻. (Insoluble salts excluded.)
Enthalpy of solution of CuSO₄; enthalpy of neutralization (strong acid + strong base); preparation of lyophilic and lyophobic sols; kinetic study of iodide ions with H₂O₂ at room temperature.
